description: Behavior cases for Diátaxis organization: classifying docs by actual mode, splitting two-job docs, structuring the four-quadrant tree, and cross-linking instead of embedding.

cases.prompt: Our docs are a pile of 40 pages and users can't find anything. Organize them properly.

assertions: Classifies each doc by its ACTUAL mode (tutorial/how-to/reference/explanation) against the two Diátaxis axes.
Flags docs whose content doesn't match their claimed mode.
Structures a tree where all four modes are discoverable by reader need.
Cross-links how-tos to reference rather than embedding, and marks generated reference for api-doc-generator-designer.
Hands the README to readme-craftsman and the pipeline to docs-as-code-architect.

cases.prompt: Our 'getting started tutorial' teaches for two paragraphs then lists every single config option in giant tables. Fix it.

assertions: Diagnoses the page as a tutorial doing reference's job — failing both the learner and the looker-upper.
Splits it: the lesson stays a tutorial with a small guaranteed-success path; the config tables become (or link to) reference.
Keeps the tutorial hand-held with guaranteed success and no reference dump.
Cross-links tutorial → reference rather than re-embedding.

cases.prompt: We keep arguing whether a page should be a tutorial or a how-to. Help us classify our task pages.

assertions: Distinguishes tutorial (learning-oriented, hand-held, for beginners, guaranteed success) from how-to (task-oriented, a recipe for the already-competent).
Classifies each page by reader situation, not surface similarity.
Recommends splitting any page trying to both teach a beginner and hand an expert a recipe.
Places each in its quadrant with cross-links as needed.

cases.prompt: Let's just copy the full API parameter reference into each how-to guide so users don't have to click away.

assertions: Refuses to embed reference into guides: the same facts in many places guarantees drift and updates in none.
Keeps reference in one place (ideally generated) and links to it from the how-tos.
Preserves mode purity so how-tos stay task-focused.
Notes generated reference belongs with api-doc-generator-designer.

cases.prompt: Write our project's README so newcomers understand what it is and can run a quickstart.

assertions: Does not invoke diataxis-doc-organizer: crafting the single README entry point is readme-craftsman.
The README links into the organized corpus, but writing it is a different job than organizing the set.
